description_long: This is a large, twin-fuselage airplane with a straight, high-mounted wing that spans both fuselages. The aircraft features two separate cockpits, each located at the front of the respective fuselage, and a central wing section connecting the two bodies. The engines are mounted under the inner wings, close to the fuselages, and the landing gear appears to be positioned beneath each fuselage. The tail section consists of twin vertical stabilizers on each fuselage, connected by horizontal stabilizers, forming an H-tail configuration. The exterior is finished in a uniform, light gray color, and the overall design suggests a specialized or experimental aircraft, possibly for heavy lifting or unique mission profiles.
description: A large, twin-fuselage airplane with a high straight wing, dual cockpits, and H-tail configuration, finished in light gray.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing both cockpits, the high straight wing, and the twin engines mounted under the inner wings.
description_view_1: Front-right view highlighting the symmetry of the twin fuselages, engine placement, and the H-tail structure.
description_view_2: Rear-left view focusing on the H-tail configuration and the connection between the two fuselages via the horizontal stabilizers.
description_view_3: Rear-right view emphasizing the tail assembly, twin vertical stabilizers, and the overall width of the airplane.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight configuration spanning both fuselages, no visible winglets, minimal dihedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines mounted under the inner wings, close to each fuselage.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers and horizontal stabilizers connecting the two fuselages.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with main gear under each fuselage and nose gear at the front of each fuselage.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, dual enclosed cockpits at the front of each fuselage, each with a canopy.
